criteria.label: Payoff concreteness
criteria.notes: Names exact 99% accurate test, 1 in 1000 disease, less-than-9% posterior, single formula.

criteria.label: Staircase coherence
criteria.notes: Seven rungs build cleanly. Minor: rung 1 conflates joint definition vs chain rule.

criteria.label: Destination precision
criteria.notes: Exact formula, P(E) expansion, proportionality role.

criteria.label: Narration clarity
criteria.notes: Symbols defined on first use. Minor ordering inconsistency for P(E).

criteria.label: Board specificity
criteria.notes: Probability tree, Venn, bar all concrete. Minor region overlap risk.

criteria.label: Board coherence
criteria.notes: Tree drawn at 5s with numbers (0.001/0.99/0.01) before narration introduces them at 145s -- forward reference. Other actions coherent.

criteria.label: Board visual balance
criteria.notes: Three distinct draws (tree, Venn, bar) plus writes/highlights. Good mix.

criteria.label: Math and notation correctness
criteria.notes: All correct. Minor: payoff says 'less than 9%' but computed 9.016% is just above.

criteria.label: Pacing and density
criteria.notes: 30s/beat. Beat at 175s combines normalizer, posterior, intuition -- slightly dense.

criteria.label: Redundancy and focus
criteria.notes: Bookended proportionality is fine. Final beat closely echoes 108s beat in wording.

topThreeIssues: Probability tree drawn at 5s with all numeric values populated before narration introduces those numbers at 145s -- creates a 2-minute forward reference; either defer numeric labels or use placeholders early
Payoff says 'less than 9%' but actual posterior is 9.016% -- slightly above 9% -- correct framing or change worked example
Beat at 175s packs normalizer computation, posterior result, and rare-prior insight into one beat -- split into two for the surprise conclusion to land
summary: High-quality plan: concrete numerical payoff, ordered staircase, three distinct visual types serving the concept. Main flaw is a multi-minute forward reference where the tree shows numbers before the narration introduces them. Minor arithmetic-vs-payoff inconsistency.
